Germany

DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students in Germany 2027: A Fully Funded Opportunity

Published 10 Jun 20265 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 10 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students in Germany 2027

The DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students in Germany 2027 is a fully funded opportunity for refugee students to pursue higher education in Germany. This scholarship program is designed to support refugee students who are seeking to continue their academic careers in Germany. By offering a fully funded scholarship, DAAD aims to provide refugee students with the opportunity to achieve their academic goals and integrate into German society.

Who Qualifies for the DAAD Scholarship

To be eligible for the DAAD Scholarship, refugee students must meet certain requirements, including holding a specific visa category, such as:

* Visa category A (asylum seeker)

* Visa category B (refugee with temporary protection)

* Visa category C (refugee with permanent protection)

* Visa category D (refugee with subsidiary protection)

* Visa category E (refugee with humanitarian protection)

Refugee students who hold one of these visa categories may be eligible to apply for the DAAD Scholarship.

Available Scholarships for Refugee Students

The following scholarships are available for refugee students in Germany:

1. DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students

The DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students offers a coverage amount of €750 per month, and is eligible for refugee students who have been accepted into a German university. The deadline for application is August 15, 2026, and the official website URL is https://www.daad.de.

2. Friedrich Ebert Foundation Scholarship

The Friedrich Ebert Foundation Scholarship offers a coverage amount of €1,000 per month, and is eligible for refugee students who are pursuing a master's or doctoral degree in Germany. The deadline for application is October 1, 2026, and the official website URL is https://www.fes.de.

3. Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship offers a coverage amount of €1,200 per month, and is eligible for refugee students who are pursuing a master's or doctoral degree in Germany. The deadline for application is November 1, 2026, and the official website URL is https://www.boell.de.

4. Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung Scholarship

The Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung Scholarship offers a coverage amount of €1,000 per month, and is eligible for refugee students who are pursuing a master's or doctoral degree in Germany. The deadline for application is December 1, 2026, and the official website URL is https://www.kas.de.

5. Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft Scholarship

The Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft Scholarship offers a coverage amount of €1,500 per month, and is eligible for refugee students who are pursuing a doctoral degree in Germany. The deadline for application is January 1, 2027, and the official website URL is https://www.dfg.de.

Application Steps for DAAD Scholarship

To apply for the DAAD Scholarship, refugee students must follow these steps:

  • Check the eligibility criteria and ensure that they meet the requirements.

  • Choose the scholarship program they wish to apply for.

  • Gather all required documents and fill out the application form.

  • Submit the application form and required documents to the DAAD office.

  • Wait for the application to be processed and reviewed.

  • Participate in an interview with the DAAD selection committee.

  • Receive notification of the application decision.

  • Begin the academic program in Germany.

Documents Checklist for DAAD Scholarship

To apply for the DAAD Scholarship, refugee students must provide the following documents:

* Passport

* Visa category document

* University admission letter

* Academic transcripts

* Language proficiency certificate

* Motivation letter

* Recommendation letter

* Proof of financial support

Frequently Asked Questions

  • What is the DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students?

The DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students is a fully funded opportunity for refugee students to pursue higher education in Germany. The scholarship program is designed to support refugee students who are seeking to continue their academic careers in Germany.

  • How can I apply for the DAAD Scholarship?

To apply for the DAAD Scholarship, refugee students must submit an application form and required documents to the DAAD office. The application form and required documents must be submitted by the deadline, and refugee students must ensure that they meet the eligibility criteria.

  • What are the eligibility criteria for the DAAD Scholarship?

The eligibility criteria for the DAAD Scholarship include holding a specific visa category, being accepted into a German university, and meeting the language proficiency requirements.

  • How much is the coverage amount for the DAAD Scholarship?

The coverage amount for the DAAD Scholarship varies depending on the scholarship program, but it can range from €750 to €1,500 per month.

  • Can I apply for multiple scholarship programs?

Yes, refugee students can apply for multiple scholarship programs, but they must ensure that they meet the eligibility criteria for each program.

  • How long does it take to process the application?

The application processing time varies depending on the scholarship program, but it can take several months to receive a decision.

  • What are the language proficiency requirements for the DAAD Scholarship?

The language proficiency requirements for the DAAD Scholarship include providing a language proficiency certificate, such as TOEFL or IELTS.

  • Can I renew my scholarship?

Yes, refugee students can renew their scholarship, but they must ensure that they are making satisfactory academic progress and meet the eligibility criteria.

External Authority Links

For more information on the DAAD Scholarship, refugee students can visit the official website of the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) at https://www.daad.de. Additionally, refugee students can visit the official website of the Federal Office for Migration and Refugees (BAMF) at https://www.bamf.de to learn more about the visa categories and eligibility criteria.

Conclusion

The DAAD Scholarship for Refugee Students in Germany 2027 is a fully funded opportunity for refugee students to pursue higher education in Germany. By offering a fully funded scholarship, DAAD aims to provide refugee students with the opportunity to achieve their academic goals and integrate into German society. To learn more about international education and cultural exchange opportunities, visit MigrantScholar.
